introSection: Swamp Buggy was a steel roller coaster built by Chance Rides previously located at AstroWorld in Houston, Texas, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
It was added in 1970 along with the Barrel of Fun as part of the new Fun Island area.

The ride was removed following the 1972 season or later.

The area the ride was in was later occupied by Viper, which opened in 1989 and replaced the Fun Island area.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride was a Chance Rides Toboggan model with additional scenery such that the central tower looked like a tree trunk.

fullText:
Swamp Buggy was a steel roller coaster built by Chance Rides previously located at AstroWorld in Houston, Texas, USA.

It was added in 1970 along with the Barrel of Fun as part of the new Fun Island area.

The ride was removed following the 1972 season or later.

The area the ride was in was later occupied by Viper, which opened in 1989 and replaced the Fun Island area.

The ride was a Chance Rides Toboggan model with additional scenery such that the central tower looked like a tree trunk.

Green track and brown supports.




 * Swamp Buggy in operation


 * 1972 Swamp Buggy advertisement
